dhulkifl_in_the_book.title: Dhu'l-Kifl mentioned in the Quran
dhulkifl_in_the_book.introduction: Dhu'l-Kifl (ذو الكفل عليه السلام) is a prophet honored by Allah in the Quran. He is mentioned alongside other messengers in Surah Al-Anbiya (21:85-86) and Surah Sad (38:48). The Quran does not detail his earthly life, but clearly affirms his place among Allah's chosen servants.
dhulkifl_in_the_book.conclusion: Dhu'l-Kifl is thus recognized among the prophets cited in the revealed Book. The believer reflects on these verses without adding to the accounts what Allah did not reveal.
dhulkifl_in_the_book.moral_lesson: This story teaches that the Quran is sufficient to honor prophets little detailed in it. We adhere to the qualities Allah attributes to them without relying on unfounded legends.
dhulkifl_in_the_book.historical_context: The Quran mentions Dhu'l-Kifl without specifying the place of his mission. Scholars advise adhering to the revealed text.

chapters.title: A mention in Al-Anbiya
chapters.content: Allah says: "And Ismail, Idris and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were steadfast." (21:85) Then: "And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous." (21:86) Dhu'l-Kifl is cited alongside other honored prophets. The Quran does not detail the circumstances of his life here, but affirms his patience, virtue and the mercy with which he was blessed.

chapters.title: A mention in Sad
chapters.content: Allah also says: "And mention Ismail, Al-Yasa and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were among the excellent." (38:48) This verse confirms Dhu'l-Kifl's lofty rank among the chosen servants (al-akhyar). The Quran presents him twice in this way, inviting the believer to honor his memory through what Allah revealed, without completing it with invented tales.

references.content: وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ كُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ ۝ وَأَدْخَلْنَـٰهُمْ فِى رَحْمَتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: And Ismail, Idris and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were steadfast. And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous.
references.relevance: First Quranic mention of Dhu'l-Kifl alongside other prophets.

references.content: وَٱذْكُرْ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَٱلْيَسَعَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ وَكُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلْأَخْيَارِ
references.translation: And mention Ismail, Al-Yasa and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were among the excellent.
references.relevance: Second Quranic mention confirming Dhu'l-Kifl's rank.

glossary.definition: Prophet mentioned in the Quran alongside other honored messengers.
Surah of the Quran where Dhu'l-Kifl is mentioned with Ismail and Idris.

dhulkifl_among_chosen.title: Dhu'l-Kifl among the excellent
dhulkifl_among_chosen.introduction: Allah honors Dhu'l-Kifl by placing him among the best servants: "And mention Ismail, Al-Yasa and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were among the excellent." (38:48) The term al-akhyar denotes excellence in piety and obedience to Allah.
dhulkifl_among_chosen.conclusion: Dhu'l-Kifl belongs to the al-akhyar, the servants chosen by Allah. This mention is sufficient to honor his memory without speculating beyond revelation.
dhulkifl_among_chosen.moral_lesson: Allah distinguishes His most pious servants. The believer aspires to excellence (khayr) in worship and good conduct.
dhulkifl_among_chosen.historical_context: The Quran does not specify the circumstances of Dhu'l-Kifl's mission. The qualifier al-akhyar suffices to affirm his lofty rank.

chapters.title: The command to mention Dhu'l-Kifl
chapters.content: Allah says: "And mention Ismail, Al-Yasa and Dhu'l-Kifl." (38:48) This command addressed to the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) shows that Dhu'l-Kifl's memory must be preserved in the Muslim community. He is cited alongside Ismail and Al-Yasa, two messengers honored in the Quran, which underscores the nobility of his place among the prophets.

chapters.title: Among the al-akhyar
chapters.content: Allah continues: "All were among the excellent (al-akhyar)." (38:48) Al-akhyar is the plural of khayr (good, excellence). It denotes those distinguished by piety, sincerity and obedience. Dhu'l-Kifl is thus counted among Allah's most excellent servants, according to what He revealed in His Word.

references.content: وَٱذْكُرْ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَٱلْيَسَعَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ وَكُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلْأَخْيَارِ
references.translation: And mention Ismail, Al-Yasa and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were among the excellent.
references.relevance: Main verse placing Dhu'l-Kifl among the al-akhyar.

glossary.definition: The excellent, the best in piety. Dhu'l-Kifl is among them according to the Quran.
Good, excellence. Root of the word al-akhyar.
Prophet cited alongside Dhu'l-Kifl in Surah Sad.

dhulkifl_steadfast.title: Dhu'l-Kifl among the steadfast
dhulkifl_steadfast.introduction: The Quran associates Dhu'l-Kifl with other exemplary prophets: "And Ismail, Idris and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were steadfast." (21:85) Allah then adds: "And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous." (21:86) These verses praise Dhu'l-Kifl's patience (sabr) and righteousness (salih).
dhulkifl_steadfast.conclusion: Dhu'l-Kifl belongs to the patient and righteous prophets. Allah granted him His mercy. He is a model of perseverance in obedience.
dhulkifl_steadfast.moral_lesson: Patience in worship and in calling to Allah is a quality of the great prophets. The believer draws inspiration from this daily.

chapters.title: Steadfast prophets
chapters.content: Allah says: "And Ismail, Idris and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were steadfast (sabirin)." (21:85) Sabr here means constancy in obedience, firmness in trials, and perseverance in the prophetic mission. Dhu'l-Kifl is cited alongside other honored messengers, showing he shares their noble qualities without the Quran detailing the circumstances of his life here.

chapters.title: Mercy and virtue
chapters.content: Allah continues: "And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous (salihin)." (21:86) Entering Allah's mercy means He granted them His grace and protection. The term salihin (righteous) confirms their uprightness. Thus, according to the Quran, Dhu'l-Kifl is a patient, righteous prophet welcomed into divine mercy.

references.content: وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ كُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
references.translation: And Ismail, Idris and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were steadfast.
references.relevance: Praises Dhu'l-Kifl's patience alongside other prophets.

references.content: وَأَدْخَلْنَـٰهُمْ فِى رَحْمَتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous.
references.relevance: Affirms Dhu'l-Kifl's virtue and the mercy Allah granted him.

glossary.definition: Steadfast, patient. Quality attributed to Dhu'l-Kifl in the Quran.
Righteous, pious. Dhu'l-Kifl is among the salihin.
Divine mercy with which Dhu'l-Kifl and the associated prophets were blessed.

dhulkifl_mercy.title: The mercy granted to Dhu'l-Kifl
dhulkifl_mercy.introduction: Allah grants special mercy to Dhu'l-Kifl and the prophets cited with him: "And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous." (21:86) This verse follows the praise of their patience (21:85) and confirms the divine grace with which they were blessed.
dhulkifl_mercy.conclusion: Dhu'l-Kifl entered Allah's mercy because of his patience and virtue. The believer sees in this the honor reserved for faithful servants.
dhulkifl_mercy.moral_lesson: Allah's mercy encompasses pious servants who persevere in obedience. The believer hopes for this mercy through patience and uprightness.
dhulkifl_mercy.historical_context: The Quran links divine mercy to the patience and virtue of the mentioned prophets, including Dhu'l-Kifl.

chapters.title: Entering Allah's mercy
chapters.content: Allah says: "And We admitted them to Our mercy (rahmatina)." (21:86) Adkhala (to admit) indicates that Allah welcomed them into His grace and protection. Dhu'l-Kifl, cited in the previous verse with Ismail and Idris as sabirin (steadfast), is included in this mercy. The Quran does not detail the form of this grace, but clearly affirms that Allah blessed them with His rahma.

chapters.title: Among the righteous
chapters.content: Allah adds: "They were indeed among the righteous (salihin)." (21:86) Divine mercy is granted to those who are salih — upright, pious and sincere in obedience. Dhu'l-Kifl shares this quality with the prophets mentioned in these verses. The believer reflects on this link between patience, virtue and mercy, without adding unrevealed tales.

references.content: وَأَدْخَلْنَـٰهُمْ فِى رَحْمَتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous.
references.relevance: Central verse on the mercy granted to Dhu'l-Kifl.

references.content: وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ كُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
references.translation: And Ismail, Idris and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were steadfast.
references.relevance: Context of patience preceding the mercy of verse 86.

glossary.definition: Divine mercy. Dhu'l-Kifl entered Allah's rahma according to the Quran.
The righteous. Dhu'l-Kifl is among them according to verse 21:86.
To admit. Allah admitted Dhu'l-Kifl and the associated prophets into His mercy.

lessons_from_dhulkifl.title: Lessons from Prophet Dhu'l-Kifl
lessons_from_dhulkifl.title_arabic: دروس من نبي الله ذي الكفل
lessons_from_dhulkifl.introduction: The Quran mentions Dhu'l-Kifl in few verses, yet each word is full of meaning: patience, virtue, excellence and mercy. Reflecting on Surah Al-Anbiya (21:85-86) and Sad (38:48), the believer draws lasting lessons without resorting to invented tales.
lessons_from_dhulkifl.conclusion: Dhu'l-Kifl remains a model of sabr, salih and excellence among the al-akhyar. His story in the Quran invites sincerity and trust in Allah's mercy.
lessons_from_dhulkifl.moral_lesson: Adhering to authentic texts protects the believer from error. Dhu'l-Kifl's qualities — patience, virtue, excellence, mercy — are goals for every pious soul.

chapters.title: Relying on the Quran
chapters.content: Allah mentions Dhu'l-Kifl only in specific verses: 21:85-86 and 38:48. This brevity teaches the Muslim not to complete revelation with legends. As with other prophets little detailed in the Quran, we honor Dhu'l-Kifl by what Allah said: sabir, salih, al-akhyar and welcomed into rahma. This is a sound method of religious knowledge.

chapters.title: Aiming for prophetic qualities
chapters.content: Sabr invites perseverance in prayer, good conduct and calling to good. The qualifier salih reminds us of the importance of uprightness. Al-akhyar calls for excellence in worship. Allah's rahma reminds us that true honor comes from the Lord. By following these teachings, the believer walks in the footsteps of honored prophets, among whom Allah counts Dhu'l-Kifl.

references.content: وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ كُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ ۝ وَأَدْخَلْنَـٰهُمْ فِى رَحْمَتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: And Ismail, Idris and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were steadfast. And We admitted them to Our mercy. They were indeed among the righteous.
references.relevance: Summary of Quranic honors granted to Dhu'l-Kifl: patience, virtue and mercy.

references.content: وَٱذْكُرْ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَٱلْيَسَعَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ وَكُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلْأَخْيَارِ
references.translation: And mention Ismail, Al-Yasa and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were among the excellent.
references.relevance: Recalls Dhu'l-Kifl's excellence among the al-akhyar.

glossary.definition: Quranic exegesis. Scholars explain verses about Dhu'l-Kifl without inventing stories.
The excellent. Dhu'l-Kifl is among them according to Quran 38:48.
